theorems.name: check_seq_hom
theorems.note: trivial: simpl unfolds ESeq to concat(map), then map_app + concat_app closes by reflexivity

theorems.name: diverged_clean_iff_barrier_free
theorems.note: key: diverged_absorbing — (if X then D else D) = D — makes Diverged mode absorbing; proof by expr_list_rect with Plist for ESeq/EApp

theorems.name: mode_monotone
theorems.note: EIf/EWhile/EFor case-split on is_varying cond; varying → inner stays Diverged in both (incl_refl); non-varying → apply IH for inner exprs

theorems.name: not_varying_converged_clean
theorems.note: helper for cdcf_check_agreement: is_varying e = false → all inner modes stay Converged → EBarrier gives []

theorems.name: cdcf_check_agreement
theorems.note: is_varying cond = false (from cdcf=false) → not_varying_converged_clean clears cond; then IH on body

theorems.name: varying_if_flags_barriers
theorems.note: is_varying cond → inner=Diverged; then diverged_clean_iff_barrier_free: ¬barrier_free → check Diverged ≠ [] → contradiction

theorems.name: superstep_outer_diverged_error
theorems.note: F-01: Diverged entry into ESuperstep with divergent=false yields BarrierError; proof by simpl + discriminate

theorems.name: env_lookup_extend_same
theorems.note: F-02 helper: env_lookup after env_extend with same key returns the stored value; proof by unfold + Nat.eqb_refl + reflexivity

theorems.name: env_let_alias_varying
theorems.note: F-02 core: if v is varying in env then ELet x v b propagates variability; proof by simpl + rewrite Hv + exact Hb

theorems.name: env_var_diverged_clean
theorems.note: F-02: EVar under Diverged mode is barrier-free (EVar carries no barrier); proof by simpl + reflexivity

theorems.name: env_check_let_alias_catches
theorems.note: F-02 soundness: varying-aliased EVar as CF condition produces BarrierError under Converged mode; proof uses env_lookup_extend_same + app_eq_nil + discriminate
